verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइति गोप्य: प्रगायन्त्य: प्रलपन्त्यश्च चित्रधा ।रुरुदु: सुस्वरं राजन् कृष्णदर्शनलालसा: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaiti gopyaḥ pragāyantyaḥpralapantyaś ca citradhāruruduḥ su-svaraṁ rājankṛṣṇa-darśana-lālasāḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: O King, having thus sung and spoken their hearts out in various charming ways, the gopīs began to weep loudly. They were very eager to see Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: तासामाविरभूच्छौरि: स्मयमानमुखाम्बुज: ।पीताम्बरधर: स्रग्वी साक्षान्मन्मथमन्मथ: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāsām āvirabhūc chauriḥsmayamāna-mukhāmbujaḥpītāmbara-dharaḥ sragvīsākṣān manmatha-manmathaḥ
verses.text: Then Lord Kṛṣṇa, a smile on His lotus face, appeared before the gopīs. Wearing a garland and a yellow garment, He directly appeared as one who can bewilder the mind of Cupid, who himself bewilders the minds of ordinary people.

verses.sanskrit: तं विलोक्यागतं प्रेष्ठं प्रीत्युत्फुल्लद‍ृशोऽबला: ।उत्तस्थुर्युगपत् सर्वास्तन्व: प्राणमिवागतम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ vilokyāgataṁ preṣṭhaṁprīty-utphulla-dṛśo ’balāḥuttasthur yugapat sarvāstanvaḥ prāṇam ivāgatam
verses.text: When the gopīs saw that their dearmost Kṛṣṇa had returned to them, they all stood up at once, and out of their affection for Him their eyes bloomed wide. It was as if the air of life had reentered their bodies.

verses.sanskrit: काचित् कराम्बुजं शौरेर्जगृहेऽञ्जलिना मुदा ।काचिद् दधार तद्बाहुमंसे चन्दनभूषितम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kācit karāmbujaṁ śaurerjagṛhe ’ñjalinā mudākācid dadhāra tad-bāhumaṁse candana-bhūṣitam
verses.text: One gopī joyfully took Kṛṣṇa’s hand between her folded palms, and another placed His arm, anointed with sandalwood paste, on her shoulder.

verses.sanskrit: काचिदञ्जलिनागृह्णात्तन्वी ताम्बूलचर्वितम् ।एका तदङ्‍‍‍‍‍घ्रिकमलं सन्तप्ता स्तनयोरधात् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kācid añjalināgṛhṇāttanvī tāmbūla-carvitamekā tad-aṅghri-kamalaṁsantaptā stanayor adhāt
verses.text: A slender gopī respectfully took in her joined hands the betel nut He had chewed, and another gopī, burning with desire, put His lotus feet on her breasts.

verses.sanskrit: एका भ्रुकुटिमाबध्य प्रेमसंरम्भविह्वला ।घ्नन्तीवैक्षत् कटाक्षेपै: सन्दष्टदशनच्छदा ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: ekā bhru-kuṭim ābadhyaprema-saṁrambha-vihvalāghnantīvaikṣat kaṭākṣepaiḥsandaṣṭa-daśana-cchadā
verses.text: One gopī, beside herself with loving anger, bit her lips and stared at Him with frowning eyebrows, as if to wound Him with her harsh glances.

verses.sanskrit: अपरानिमिषद्‌दृग्भ्यां जुषाणा तन्मुखाम्बुजम् ।आपीतमपि नातृप्यत् सन्तस्तच्चरणं यथा ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: aparānimiṣad-dṛgbhyāṁjuṣāṇā tan-mukhāmbujamāpītam api nātṛpyatsantas tac-caraṇaṁ yathā
verses.text: Another gopī looked with unblinking eyes upon His lotus face, but even after deeply relishing its sweetness She did not feel satiated, just as mystic saints are never satiated when meditating upon the Lord’s feet.

verses.sanskrit: तं काचिन्नेत्ररन्ध्रेण हृदिकृत्वा निमील्य च ।पुलकाङ्‌‌ग्युपगुह्यास्ते योगीवानन्द सम्प्लुता ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ kācin netra-randhreṇahṛdi kṛtvā nimīlya capulakāṅgy upaguhyāsteyogīvānanda-samplutā
verses.text: One gopī took the Lord through the aperture of her eyes and placed Him within her heart. Then, with her eyes closed and her bodily hairs standing on end, she continuously embraced Him within. Thus immersed in transcendental ecstasy, she resembled a yogī meditating upon the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वास्ता: केशवालोकपरमोत्सवनिर्वृता: ।जहुर्विरहजं तापं प्राज्ञं प्राप्य यथा जना: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarvās tāḥ keśavāloka-paramotsava-nirvṛtāḥjahur viraha-jaṁ tāpaṁprājñaṁ prāpya yathā janāḥ
verses.text: All the gopīs enjoyed the greatest festivity when they saw their beloved Keśava again. They gave up the distress of separation, just as people in general forget their misery when they gain the association of a spiritually enlightened person.

verses.sanskrit: ताभिर्विधूतशोकाभिर्भगवानच्युतो वृत: ।व्यरोचताधिकं तात पुरुष: शक्तिभिर्यथा ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: tābhir vidhūta-śokābhirbhagavān acyuto vṛtaḥvyarocatādhikaṁ tātapuruṣaḥ śaktibhir yathā
verses.text: Encircled by the gopīs, who were now relieved of all distress, Lord Acyuta, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, shone forth splendidly. My dear King, Kṛṣṇa thus appeared like the Supersoul encircled by His spiritual potencies.

verses.sanskrit: ता: समादाय कालिन्द्या निर्विश्य पुलिनं विभु: ।विकसत्कुन्दमन्दारसुरभ्यनिलषट्पदम् ॥ ११ ॥शरच्चन्द्रांशुसन्दोहध्वस्तदोषातम: शिवम् ।कृष्णाया हस्ततरलाचितकोमलवालुकम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāḥ samādāya kālindyānirviśya pulinaṁ vibhuḥvikasat-kunda-mandārasurabhy-anila-ṣaṭpadam
verses.text: The almighty Lord then took the gopīs with Him to the bank of the Kālindī, who with the hands of her waves had scattered piles of soft sand upon the shore. In that auspicious place the breeze, bearing the fragrance of blooming kunda and mandāra flowers, attracted many bees, and the abundant rays of the autumn moon dispelled the darkness of night.

verses.sanskrit: तद्दर्शनाह्लादविधूतहृद्रुजोमनोरथान्तं श्रुतयो यथा ययु: ।स्वैरुत्तरीयै: कुचकुङ्कुमाङ्कितै-रचीक्लृपन्नासनमात्मबन्धवे ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tad-darśanāhlāda-vidhūta-hṛd-rujomanorathāntaṁ śrutayo yathā yayuḥsvair uttarīyaiḥ kuca-kuṅkumāṅkitairacīkḷpann āsanam ātma-bandhave
verses.text: Their heartache vanquished by the ecstasy of seeing Kṛṣṇa, the gopīs, like the personified Vedas before them, felt their desires completely fulfilled. For their dear friend Kṛṣṇa they arranged a seat with their shawls which were smeared with the kuṅkuma powder from their breasts.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रोपविष्टो भगवान् स ईश्वरोयोगेश्वरान्तर्हृदि कल्पितासन: ।चकास गोपीपरिषद्गतोऽर्चित-स्त्रैलोक्यलक्ष्म्येकपदं वपुर्दधत् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatropaviṣṭo bhagavān sa īśvaroyogeśvarāntar-hṛdi kalpitāsanaḥcakāsa gopī-pariṣad-gato ’rcitastrailokya-lakṣmy-eka-padaṁ vapur dadhat
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, for whom the great masters of mystic meditation arrange a seat within their hearts, took His seat in the assembly of gopīs. His transcendental body, the exclusive abode of beauty and opulence within the three worlds, shone brilliantly as the gopīs worshiped Him.

verses.sanskrit: सभाजयित्वा तमनङ्गदीपनंसहासलीलेक्षणविभ्रमभ्रुवा ।संस्पर्शनेनाङ्ककृताङ्‍‍‍‍‍घ्रिहस्तयो:संस्तुत्य ईषत्कुपिता बभाषिरे ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sabhājayitvā tam anaṅga-dīpanaṁsahāsa-līlekṣaṇa-vibhrama-bhruvāsaṁsparśanenāṅka-kṛtāṅghri-hastayoḥsaṁstutya īṣat kupitā babhāṣire
verses.text: Śrī Kṛṣṇa had awakened romantic desires within the gopīs, and they honored Him by glancing at Him with playful smiles, gesturing amorously with their eyebrows, and massaging His hands and feet as they held them in their laps. Even while worshiping Him, however, they felt somewhat angry, and thus they addressed Him as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीगोप्य ऊचु:भजतोऽनुभजन्त्येक एक एतद्विपर्ययम् ।नोभयांश्च भजन्त्येक एतन्नो ब्रूहि साधु भो: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-gopya ūcuḥbhajato ’nubhajanty ekaeka etad-viparyayamnobhayāṁś ca bhajanty ekaetan no brūhi sādhu bhoḥ
verses.text: The gopīs said: Some people reciprocate the affection only of those who are affectionate toward them, while others show affection even to those who are indifferent or inimical. And yet others will not show affection toward anyone. Dear Kṛṣṇa, please properly explain this matter to us.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचमिथो भजन्ति ये सख्य: स्वार्थैकान्तोद्यमा हि ते ।न तत्र सौहृदं धर्म: स्वार्थार्थं तद्धि नान्यथा ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācamitho bhajanti ye sakhyaḥsvārthaikāntodyamā hi tena tatra sauhṛdaṁ dharmaḥsvārthārthaṁ tad dhi nānyathā
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: So-called friends who show affection for each other only to benefit themselves are actually selfish. They have no true friendship, nor are they following the true principles of religion. Indeed, if they did not expect benefit for themselves, they would not reciprocate.

verses.sanskrit: भजन्त्यभजतो ये वै करुणा: पितरौ यथा ।धर्मो निरपवादोऽत्र सौहृदं च सुमध्यमा: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhajanty abhajato ye vaikaruṇāḥ pitarau yathādharmo nirapavādo ’trasauhṛdaṁ ca su-madhyamāḥ
verses.text: My dear slender-waisted gopīs, some people are genuinely merciful or, like parents, naturally affectionate. Such persons, who devotedly serve even those who fail to reciprocate with them, are following the true, faultless path of religion, and they are true well-wishers.

verses.sanskrit: भजतोऽपि न वै केचिद् भजन्त्यभजत: कुत: ।आत्मारामा ह्याप्तकामा अकृतज्ञा गुरुद्रुह: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhajato ’pi na vai kecidbhajanty abhajataḥ kutaḥātmārāmā hy āpta-kāmāakṛta-jñā guru-druhaḥ
verses.text: Then there are those individuals who are spiritually self-satisfied, materially fulfilled or by nature ungrateful or simply envious of superiors. Such persons will not love even those who love them, what to speak of those who are inimical.

verses.sanskrit: नाहं तु सख्यो भजतोऽपि जन्तून्भजाम्यमीषामनुवृत्तिवृत्तये ।यथाधनो लब्धधने विनष्टेतच्चिन्तयान्यन्निभृतो न वेद ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: nāhaṁ tu sakhyo bhajato ’pi jantūnbhajāmy amīṣām anuvṛtti-vṛttayeyathādhano labdha-dhane vinaṣṭetac-cintayānyan nibhṛto na veda
verses.text: But the reason I do not immediately reciprocate the affection of living beings even when they worship Me, O gopīs, is that I want to intensify their loving devotion. They then become like a poor man who has gained some wealth and then lost it, and who thus becomes so anxious about it that he can think of nothing else.

verses.sanskrit: एवं मदर्थोज्झितलोकवेद-स्वानां हि वो मय्यनुवृत्तयेऽबला: ।मयापरोक्षं भजता तिरोहितंमासूयितुं मार्हथ तत् प्रियं प्रिया: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ mad-arthojjhita-loka-vedasvānām hi vo mayy anuvṛttaye ’balāḥmayāparokṣaṁ bhajatā tirohitaṁmāsūyituṁ mārhatha tat priyaṁ priyāḥ
verses.text: My dear girls, understanding that simply for My sake you had rejected the authority of worldly opinion, of the Vedas and of your relatives, I acted as I did only to increase your attachment to Me. Even when I removed Myself from your sight by suddenly disappearing, I never stopped loving you. Therefore, My beloved gopīs, please do not harbor any bad feelings toward Me, your beloved.

verses.sanskrit: न पारयेऽहं निरवद्यसंयुजांस्वसाधुकृत्यं विबुधायुषापि व: ।या माभजन् दुर्जरगेहश‍ृङ्खला:संवृश्च्य तद् व: प्रतियातु साधुना ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: na pāraye ’haṁ niravadya-saṁyujāṁsva-sādhu-kṛtyaṁ vibudhāyuṣāpi vaḥyā mābhajan durjara-geha-śṛṅkhalāḥsaṁvṛścya tad vaḥ pratiyātu sādhunā
verses.text: I am not able to repay My debt for your spotless service, even within a lifetime of Brahmā. Your connection with Me is beyond reproach. You have worshiped Me, cutting off all domestic ties, which are difficult to break. Therefore please let your own glorious deeds be your compensation.
